trade.summary: Thailandia exports electronic components, automobiles, food products (rice, seafood), and rubber to the EAU. The EAU exports crude oil, refined petroleum, and gold to Thailandia. The EAU is an important transit hub for Thai goods entering the Middle East and Africa.

diplomatic.summary: Relations are friendly with growing commercial and tourism ties. Thailandia has a large migrant worker community in the EAU. Thai tourism to EAU has grown. Both countries cooperate in ASEAN-GCC forums. High-level visits are regular.

military.summary: Limited military ties. Some defense industrial contacts. Thailandia imports some EAU military technology. No significant joint exercises or arms transfer programs.

financial.summary: EAU sovereign funds have invested in Thai infrastructure and real estate. Thai SET-listed companies have EAU business interests. Remittances from Thai workers in EAU are significant. Dubai is an important financial gateway for Thai businesses entering Middle East.

energy.summary: Thailandia imports EAU crude oil and refined petroleum. PTTEP (Thailandia's state oil company) has exploration interests in the Gulf. Thailandia's energy import dependence makes EAU supply relationships strategically important.

scientific.summary: Scientific collaboration is modest. Thailandia and EAU cooperate in food technology, agricultural science, and some medical research. Tourism and hospitality management education links are growing.
